     The Numbers Business Report

Wanderlust (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $17,288,155Details
International Box Office $6,871,779
Worldwide Box Office $24,159,934
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $4,816,783
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $922,124
Total $5,738,907
Further financial details...

Synopsis

George and Linda are an overextended, stressed out Manhattan couple. After George is downsized out of his job, they find themselves with only one option: to move in with George's awful brother in Atlanta. On the way there, George and Linda stumble upon Elysium, an idyllic community populated by colorful characters who embrace a different way of looking at things. Money? It can't buy happiness. Careers? Who needs them? Clothes? Only if you want them Is Elysium the fresh start George and Linda need? Or will the change of perspective cause more problems than it solves?

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$6,526,650 (37.8% of total gross)
Legs:2.65 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:71.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$32,500,000 (worldwide box office is 0.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,002 opening theaters/2,002 max. theaters, 3.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $25,975,673

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: February 24th, 2012 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: April 12th, 2012 (Wide) (Ukraine)
April 13th, 2012 (Wide), released as Locura en el paraíso (Mexico)
May 10th, 2012 (Wide) (Croatia)
May 30th, 2012 (Wide) (France)
June 15th, 2012 (Wide) (Spain)
Video Release: June 19th, 2012 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for sexual content, graphic nudity, language and drug use
(Rating bulletin 2178, 6/29/2011)
Running Time: 98 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble, Improvised, Outtakes During Credits
Plot point: Country Mouse, City Mouse, Fired, Infidelity, Land Developer, Mid-Life Crisis, Narcotics, Political, Romance
Social setting: Counterculture, New Age
Subgenre: Farcical / Slapstick Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Universal Pictures, Relativity Media, Apatow Productions, Hot Dog
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

George and Linda are an overextended, stressed out Manhattan couple. After George is downsized out of his job, they find themselves with only one option: to move in with George's awful brother in Atlanta. On the way there, George and Linda stumble upon Elysium, an idyllic community populated by colorful characters who embrace a different way of looking at things. Money? It can't buy happiness. Careers? Who needs them? Clothes? Only if you want them Is Elysium the fresh start George and Linda need? Or will the change of perspective cause more problems than it solves?
